summary: James is a 28-year-old market trader from Lagos who sources electronics from Alaba International Market and sells across 3 states. He manages a 6-person distribution network, speaks 4 languages, and coordinates logistics via WhatsApp.

skills.evidence: Uses Igbo with Alaba Market suppliers

skills.source_activity: Negotiating bulk prices at Alaba Market
Knowing which electronics sell before rainy season vs dry season
Identifying price gaps between Lagos and Kano markets
Deciding which customers can buy on credit
Trading with Igbo, Hausa and Yoruba communities
Managing distributor network via WhatsApp groups

intake_transcript.question: What do you spend most of your time doing each day?
intake_transcript.answer: I buy electronics from Alaba Market in Lagos and sell them. I have distributors in Abuja and Kano who take stock from me.

intake_transcript.question: What skills have you learned on your own?
intake_transcript.answer: I learned how to read which products will sell. Like before the rainy season, generators move fast. I also manage my distributors through WhatsApp groups I created.

intake_transcript.question: What languages do you speak?
intake_transcript.answer: Yoruba is my mother tongue. I speak Igbo to deal with Alaba suppliers. I speak Pidgin with everyone. And English for writing.

intake_transcript.question: Have you ever taught someone or organized people?
intake_transcript.answer: I trained my two younger cousins to handle the Lagos delivery side. I also run the Kano distributor group and I resolve disputes when they have problems.

intake_transcript.question: What tools or technology do you use?
intake_transcript.answer: Smartphone, WhatsApp, POS machine for card payments. I use Excel to track which distributors owe me money.
